_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Determine and develop leads and identify sources of information in order to identify and/or prosecute the responsible parties to an intrusion or other crimes.
reference_document_element_description: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-T-010 (AB, cosine: 0.486), DG-T-003 (DG, cosine: 0.479).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.9/10 (raw 0.287). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [crimes, determine, develop, identify, information, intrusion] vs WIDAI-only terms [analysis, analytical, business, data, delivery, documented] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.853 (P=0.842, R=0.864). R exceeds P by 0.022 — the WIDAI KSA's 18 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 24 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Determine and develop leads and identify sources of information in order to iden" and "Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extractio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.518 > STS 0.287 (gap +0.230). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.6%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [crimes, determine, develop, identify, information, intrusion]. WIDAI terms: [analysis, analytical, business, data, delivery, documented].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.230 with low STS 0.287. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
